creator: freebsd@simplerezo.com
text:
FreeBSD 6.2, with a non-GENERIC kernel, running as a guest operating
system in VMware is very unstable.

Boot process is slow and there is lot of messages : 'calcru: runtime
went backwards from xxxx[1] usec to xxxx[1] usec for pid <pid> (<process>)'. 

When it has booted, system's time is blocked and after few seconds system
freezes and does not respond.


[1] : some numbers

Fix: 

In the kernel configuration, disable both options 'CPU_FASTER_5X86_FPU',
and 'AUTO_EOI_1'
How-To-Repeat: Install FreeBSD in VMware, and use a custom kernel built from
/usr/src/sys/i386/conf/NOTES
